title: Intricately Broiled Japanese Pickles Skewers
description: A traditional Japanese dish featuring pickles, broiled to perfection and served on a banana leaf, this recipe is a must try for its unique blend of flavors and textures. It is a jain-friendly and festive dish, perfect for special occasions.
instructions: Preheat the broiler to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together mustard paste, soy sauce, sesame oil, salt, and black pepper.
Add the sliced Japanese pickles to the bowl and toss to coat with the mustard mixture.
Thread 2-3 pickles onto each banana leaf, leaving a small space between each pickle.
Brush the banana leaves with vegetable oil and place under the broiler for 5-7 minutes, or until the pickles are slightly charred.
Remove the skewers from the oven and sprinkle with sesame seeds and chopped green onions.
Serve the skewers hot, garnished with additional green onions if desired.
Repeat the process with the remaining pickles and banana leaves.
Arrange the skewers on a platter and serve immediately.
